gene_description: GABA(A) receptor subunit alpha 6 — component of GABA(A) chloride channels primarily expressed in cerebellar granule cells. Variants may influence benzodiazepine sensitivity, seizure susceptibility, and stress response through altered GABAergic neurotransmission.

source.title: Association of GABRA6 1519 T>C (rs3219151) and Synapsin II (rs37733634) gene polymorphisms with the development of idiopathic generalized epilepsy
source.finding: GABRA6 rs3219151 C-allele associated with increased epilepsy susceptibility in some populations (South India OR=3.6, 95% CI 1.8-7.2); T-allele associated with greater stress response and potentially heightened benzodiazepine sensitivity.

CC.phenotype: Homozygous C-allele (high-risk variant in some populations)
CC.effect: Associated with altered GABRA6 function and increased seizure risk in Asian populations (OR~3.6). May indicate reduced GABAergic inhibition in cerebellum and brainstem. Potentially altered benzodiazepine sensitivity; some individuals may show enhanced response, others may have reduced therapeutic efficacy. Elevated baseline anxiety and stress responsivity reported.
CC.recommendation: Baseline EEG and seizure risk assessment recommended, particularly if Asian ancestry. For benzodiazepines: start with lower doses and titrate slowly; monitor for paradoxical reactions or inadequate response. Enhanced monitoring for seizure development. Consider alternative anxiolytics if benzodiazepine response suboptimal.

CT.effect: Intermediate phenotype. Reduced seizure risk compared to CC genotype. Variable benzodiazepine sensitivity; most individuals show standard response, some may show enhanced sensitivity.
CT.recommendation: Standard benzodiazepine dosing with assessment of therapeutic response. Monitor for both efficacy and adverse effects. Standard seizure precautions as clinically indicated.

TC.effect: Intermediate phenotype. Reduced seizure risk compared to CC genotype. Variable benzodiazepine sensitivity; most individuals show standard response, some may show enhanced sensitivity.
TC.recommendation: Standard benzodiazepam dosing with assessment of therapeutic response. Monitor for both efficacy and adverse effects. Standard seizure precautions as clinically indicated.

TT.effect: Associated with normal to reduced seizure risk. T-allele carriers show greater physiological and endocrine stress response, potentially indicating increased benzodiazepine sensitivity in some individuals. Standard to enhanced GABA(A) function.
TT.recommendation: Standard benzodiazepine dosing for most individuals; monitor clinical response. Those with elevated baseline stress response may benefit from lower initial doses with careful titration.
